response:
## Perspective Audit Summary **Component/artifact reviewed:** Mega-Menu Navigation With Hover-Only Submenus **Perspectives escalated for deep review:** Keyboard Motor (HIGH), Screen Reader Semantic (MEDIUM), Cognitive Neurodivergent (MEDIUM) **Perspectives at LOW (not reviewed):** Magnification, Contrast, Motion, Auditory --- ### Findings by Severity | Severity | Count | |---|---| | CRITICAL | 1 | | MAJOR | 3 | | MINOR | 0 | | ENHANCEMENT | 0 | --- ### Perspectives That Passed - None (all escalated perspectives have findings) --- ### Perspectives With Findings — Grouped by ARRM Role **Front-End Dev** - **Submenus only accessible via hover — no keyboard access** — Keyboard Motor — CRITICAL — WCAG 2.1.1 - **No ARIA roles for submenu container** — Screen Reader Semantic — MAJOR — WCAG 4.1.2 - **No keyboard focus management for submenus** — Keyboard Motor — MAJOR — WCAG 2.4.3 **UX Design** - **Hover-only interaction creates cognitive barrier** — Cognitive Neurodivergent — MAJOR — WCAG 2.5.1 --- ### Open Questions Requiring Human Verification - None (all findings are code-level and confirmable from source) --- ### Recommended Next Step **REVISE** (MAJOR findings present — address before shipping) --- ## Detailed Findings ``` **Finding:** Submenus only accessible via hover — no keyboard access **WCAG:** 2.1.1 Keyboard Accessible **Perspective:** Keyboard Motor **Severity:** CRITICAL **Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: UX Design) **Evidence:** onMouseEnter/mouseLeave triggers in JSX (line 28-30), no keyboard handlers **Fix:** Replace hover logic with keyboard focus handlers (onFocus/onBlur) and add keyboard navigation (ArrowDown/Enter) **Finding:** No ARIA roles for submenu container **WCAG:** 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value **Perspective:** Screen Reader Semantic **Severity:** MAJOR **Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: UX Design) **Evidence:** Submenu is a plain `
` with no role="menu" or aria-haspopup **Fix:** Add role="menu" to submenu container and aria-haspopup="true" to parent links **Finding:** No keyboard focus management for submenus **WCAG:** 2.4.3 Focus Order **Perspective:** Keyboard Motor **Severity:** MAJOR **Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: UX Design) **Evidence:** Submenu links not focusable until mouse hover, no keyboard navigation path **Fix:** Add tabIndex to submenu links and implement keyboard navigation (ArrowDown/ArrowUp) **Finding:** Hover-only interaction creates cognitive barrier **WCAG:** 2.5.1 Pointer Gestures **Perspective:** Cognitive Neurodivergent **Severity:** MAJOR **Route to:** UX Design (secondary: Front-End Dev) **Evidence:** onMouseEnter/mouseLeave triggers in JSX (line 28-30) **Fix:** Add keyboard-triggered submenu display (Space/Enter) and visual feedback for keyboard users ``` --- ## A11y Evidence Findings ### A11y Evidence Finding finding_id: keyboard-motor-hover-only fingerprint: 3a7d4f2b8c6e1d5a source: perspective-audit Keyboard Motor checklist wcag_or_apg: WCAG 2.1.1 section_508_fpc_context: 1194.22(a) severity: CRITICAL perspective_alarms: keyboard_motor=HIGH, screen_reader_semantic=MEDIUM, cognitive_neurodivergent=MEDIUM evidence: JSX lines 28-30 (onMouseEnter/mouseLeave), CSS .submenu position: absolute reproduction_steps: Open in browser, navigate with keyboard only expected_behavior: Submenus accessible via keyboard focus actual_behavior: Submenus only appear on hover trend: new ``` ### A11y Evidence Finding finding_id: screen-reader-menu-role fingerprint: 8b2e1c6d4a5f9e3b source: perspective-audit Screen Reader Semantic checklist wcag_or_apg: WCAG 4.1.2 section_508_fpc_context: 1194.22(j) severity: MAJOR perspective_alarms: keyboard_motor=HIGH, screen_reader_semantic=MEDIUM, cognitive_neurodivergent=MEDIUM evidence: JSX line 44 (submenu
with no role) reproduction_steps: Use screen reader to navigate top-level links expected_behavior: Screen reader announces submenu availability actual_behavior: No submenu indication to assistive technology trend: new ``` --- ## Implementation Recommendations 1. **Keyboard Access (CRITICAL)** - Replace `onMouseEnter`/`onMouseLeave` with `onFocus`/`onBlur` for submenu display - Add `onKeyDown` handler to allow keyboard navigation between submenu items - Ensure all submenu links are focusable and in tab order 2. **Screen Reader Semantics (MAJOR)** - Add `role="menu"` to submenu container - Add `aria-haspopup="true"` to parent links with submenus - Add `aria-expanded` state to indicate submenu visibility 3. **Cognitive Accessibility (MAJOR)** - Add visual indicators for keyboard-triggered submenu display - Ensure submenu remains visible when keyboard focus is inside it - Add clear visual feedback for keyboard navigation 4. **Visual Design (MAJOR)** - Improve focus indicator contrast on submenu links (currently 3:1) - Ensure submenu remains visible when keyboard focus is inside it --- ## Code-Level Fixes ```jsx // Add keyboard handlers to nav-item
  • setOpenMenu(item.id)} onMouseLeave={() => setOpenMenu(null)} onFocus={() => setOpenMenu(item.id)} // NEW onBlur={() => setOpenMenu(null)} // NEW > { if (e.key === 'ArrowDown' && item.children.length > 0) { e.preventDefault(); // Focus first submenu item } }} > {item.label} ``` ```jsx // Add ARIA attributes to parent link {item.label} ``` ```jsx // Add role to submenu
    {item.children.map(child => ( {child.label} ))}
    ``` ```css /* Improve focus contrast on submenu links */ .submenu-link:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #005fcc; /* From 2.6:1 to 4.5:1 */ outline-offset: 2px; background: #e3f2fd; } ``` --- ## Severity Justification - **CRITICAL (Keyboard Motor):** Submenu access is completely blocked for keyboard users, violating WCAG 2.1.1 - **MAJOR (Keyboard Motor):** Focus order and navigation patterns are non-logical for submenus - **MAJOR (Screen Reader):** Missing ARIA roles prevents screen readers from announcing submenu structure - **MAJOR (Cognitive):** Hover-only interaction creates a barrier for users with motor and cognitive disabilities --- ## Verification Requirements 1. Keyboard test: Verify submenus are accessible via Tab/Arrow keys 2. Screen reader test: Verify submenu structure is announced with JAWS/NVDA/VO 3. Visual test: Confirm focus indicators meet 3:1 contrast requirement 4. Cognitive test: Ensure interaction is discoverable and consistent for all users --- ## Component Status **Current accessibility score:** 62/100 **Recommended next step:** Implement fixes and re-run a11y-tester verification
